title: Scottish Highlands Discovery.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scenic scheduled trains between cities, with private taxi transfers at each end.

transport.1: Edinburgh airport to Edinburgh hotel private taxi transfer.
transport.3: Edinburgh to Inverness scheduled train, standard class, skirting the Cairngorms and Loch Ness.
transport.5: Inverness to Fort William scheduled coach transfer via Fort Augustus and the Great Glen.
transport.6: Fort William to Glasgow on the scenic West Highland Line train, past Glencoe and Loch Lomond.
transport.7: Glasgow hotel to Glasgow airport private taxi transfer.

inclusions: Edinburgh airport to hotel private taxi transfer on arrival
Edinburgh to Inverness train ticket, standard class
Inverness to Fort William coach transfer via the Great Glen
Fort William to Glasgow train ticket on the West Highland Line
Glasgow hotel to airport private taxi transfer on departure

selfdrive.name: Self Drive
selfdrive.blurb: Rental car from arrival to departure — explore the Highlands at your own pace.

transport.1: Pick up rental car at Edinburgh airport — SUV class, Ford Kuga or similar.
transport.3: Drive Edinburgh to Inverness at your own pace, with stops at Loch Ness and Urquhart Castle.
transport.5: Drive Inverness to Fort William via Fort Augustus and the Great Glen at your own pace.
transport.6: Drive Fort William to Glasgow via Glencoe and Loch Lomond, with suggested photo stops.
transport.7: Return rental car at Glasgow airport before departure.

inclusions: Rental car for 7 days, SUV class Ford Kuga or similar
Fully comprehensive insurance and unlimited mileage
Self-guided route notes with recommended stops at Loch Ness, Glencoe and Loch Lomond
One-way drop-off from Edinburgh to Glasgow airport

private.name: Private Exclusive Coach
private.blurb: A private vehicle on disposal from Edinburgh to Glasgow, ideal for families and small groups.

transport.1: Edinburgh airport to Edinburgh hotel private transfer.
transport.2: Edinburgh panoramic tour, 3 hrs, with private vehicle.
transport.3: Private transfer Edinburgh to Inverness, en route Loch Ness and the Highlands. Vehicle on disposal from today until Day 6.
transport.4: Skye excursion by private vehicle, via Eilean Donan Castle.
transport.5: Private transfer Inverness to Fort William via Fort Augustus and the Great Glen, using the vehicle on disposal.
transport.6: Private transfer Fort William to Glasgow, en route Glencoe and Loch Lomond.
transport.7: Glasgow hotel to Glasgow airport private transfer.

inclusions: Private vehicle on disposal from Day 3 to Day 6 (8 hrs per day)
Edinburgh airport to hotel private transfer on arrival
Edinburgh panoramic tour, 3 hrs, by private vehicle
Private transfer Edinburgh to Inverness via Loch Ness and the Highlands
Private transfer Fort William to Glasgow via Glencoe and Loch Lomond
Glasgow hotel to airport private transfer on departure

days.title: Arrive Edinburgh
days.desc: Arrive in the Scottish capital, dominated by Edinburgh Castle on its volcanic crag. The rest of the day is free to wander the Royal Mile and Princes Street Gardens, an easy walk from the city-centre hotels.
days.taste: A dram of single malt Scotch whisky at Deacon Brodie's Tavern, a Royal Mile institution licensed since 1806 and named after the city councillor and secret burglar said to have inspired Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde.

days.title: Edinburgh
days.desc: A full day to explore Edinburgh: the Castle esplanade and Crown Jewels, the Royal Mile down to the Palace of Holyroodhouse, and the climb up Arthur's Seat for views over the whole city.
days.experience: Descend into the Real Mary King's Close, the hidden 17th-century Old Town streets sealed beneath the modern city.

days.title: Edinburgh → Inverness
days.desc: Head north through Perthshire and the Cairngorms towards the Great Glen, skirting the shores of Loch Ness. Time permitting, stop at the ruins of Urquhart Castle, one of the most famously photographed views of the loch.
days.taste: The route passes close to Glenturret Distillery near Crieff, licensed since 1763 and generally regarded as Scotland's oldest working distillery, now home to the Famous Grouse Experience.

days.title: Skye Excursion
days.desc: A day trip west to the Isle of Skye, passing the postcard-famous Eilean Donan Castle on its islet before crossing to Skye's Cuillin mountains and the harbour town of Portree.
days.taste: Skye is home to the Talisker distillery, the island's only whisky maker, founded in 1830 on the shores of Loch Harport.

days.title: Inverness → Fort William
days.desc: Travel south along the Great Glen via the locks of the Caledonian Canal at Fort Augustus, arriving beneath Ben Nevis, the highest peak in the British Isles at 1,345 metres.
days.taste: Fort William is home to Ben Nevis Distillery, founded at the foot of the mountain in 1825 and one of the oldest licensed distilleries in the Highlands.

days.title: Fort William → Glasgow
days.desc: Continue south through the dramatic glacial valley of Glencoe before following the bonnie banks of Loch Lomond, the largest lake by surface area in Great Britain, on into Glasgow.
days.taste: Glasgow lays a well-known claim to inventing chicken tikka masala in one of its Asian restaurants in the 1970s — still a fixture on menus across the city's curry houses.
days.shopping: Glasgow's Buchanan Street and the Style Mile make it the UK's second-largest shopping destination after London's West End.

days.title: Depart Glasgow
days.desc: Free time until your transfer to Glasgow airport, depending on flight time — the Kelvingrove Art Gallery and Glasgow Cathedral are both within easy reach of the city-centre hotels.

hotels.h3: Holiday Inn Express Edinburgh City Centre
Holiday Inn Express Glasgow - City Ctr Riverside

about.title: Best season
about.body: May to September for long daylight hours and the best chance of settled Highland weather; shoulder months are quieter.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Cool and changeable throughout the year, roughly 10–18°C in summer — pack a waterproof layer regardless of season.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Pound sterling (£) throughout; the UK is outside the Schengen area, so standard UK entry requirements apply for visitors.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Book Edinburgh Castle tickets ahead in summer, especially during the Festival in August. Loch Ness boat trips and Urquhart Castle are quietest early morning.

goodToKnow.title: Driving
goodToKnow.body: The UK drives on the left. Highland roads north and west of Fort William are often single-track with passing places — allow extra time.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: The Fort William to Glasgow leg runs on the West Highland Line, rated one of the world's great scenic railways — window seats are worth requesting.

goodToKnow.title: Your vehicle
goodToKnow.body: Driver working hours are limited by law, so day-trip timings on the Highlands route are planned around rest breaks. Luggage space fits one large case per guest.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Layers and a waterproof jacket for any month, plus sturdy shoes for castle grounds and lochside paths. Midge repellent is worth packing for the Highland legs between late May and September, when the biting midge is at its most active.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards and contactless are accepted almost everywhere, though a little cash is useful in rural Highland villages. Tipping around 10% in restaurants is customary, not obligatory.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
